documentation.value: An XML element with an opening tag, optional attributes, content, and a closing tag.
The tag name (must start with a letter or underscore, no spaces)
Optional name-value pairs providing metadata about the element
The element content: text, child elements, or mixed content
An attribute provides additional information about an element. Values must be quoted with single or double quotes.
The attribute name
The attribute value (must be enclosed in quotes)
A CDATA section allows inclusion of text that would otherwise be recognized as markup. Content is not parsed.
Unparsed character data — can contain `<`, `>`, `&` without escaping
A processing instruction provides instructions to an application processing the XML document.
The target application name (e.g. `xml`, `xml-stylesheet`)
Additional data for the target application
Declares an XML namespace, associating a prefix with a URI to avoid name collisions between elements from different vocabularies.
The namespace prefix to use (omit for default namespace: `xmlns="..."`)
The URI that uniquely identifies the namespace
An XML comment. Comments cannot contain `--` and cannot be nested.
The comment text (must not contain double hyphens `--`)
A character or entity reference. Predefined entities: `<` (<), `>` (>), `&` (&), `'` ('), `"` (").
An entity name (`lt`, `gt`, `amp`, `apos`, `quot`) or numeric reference (`#60`, `#x3C`)
The XML declaration specifies the XML version, character encoding, and whether the document is standalone.
The XML version number (`1.0` or `1.1`)
The character encoding (e.g. `UTF-8`, `UTF-16`, `ISO-8859-1`)
`yes` if the document has no external markup declarations, `no` otherwise
